text:
is the son of King Bradley, the Führer of Amestris.

History

In the 2003 anime, he is merely the Führer's timid adopted child, appearing late in the series.

On his tenth birthday, young Selim receives a model train and discovers the skull that constitutes Pride's remains and is trusted by his father with guarding it, calling it his treasure. Delighted, he gladly takes the responsibility in order to make his father proud of him. When the Bradleys hear a faint thud coming from their cellar, King Bradley has his guards send his wife and son to the command center for their safety. On the way, the car carrying Selim and his mother is hit by Lt. Frank Archer, knocking the driver and Mrs. Bradley unconscious. Selim, worried about his father and wanting to make sure that his secret treasure is safe, leaves the safety of the car and heads back to his home. After retrieving his father's treasure and putting it in his bag, Selim accidentally wanders onto the scene of King and Roy Mustang's fight. As a Homunculus, Pride is severely weakened by the proximity of his human remains, allowing Mustang to gain the upper hand and remove the sword piercing his shoulder. In a fit of rage, Pride strangles Selim until his neck makes a snapping sound, saying that all of humanity is worthless as he crudely tosses Selim aside. After using the skull to kill the Homunculus, Mustang picks up Selim and carries him outside the burning manor. Selim is last seen besides Mustang while Hawkeye cries over the perceived death of Mustang.

Selim's ultimate fate is left ambiguous, as he is never seen nor mentioned after being carried out of the burning manor. An audible snapping sound can be heard shortly before he's dropped to the ground by Pride, indicating his neck was broken. Contrary to popular belief, a broken neck isn't immediately fatal, so it's very possible he survived. This would largely depend on the specifics of his injury, as injuries to the spinal cord are significantly more fatal than injuries to other parts of the neck. Assuming his spine was undamaged, he would in theory be able to make a full recovery if given timely medical assistance, or per. As Hawkeye found Selim and Mustang almost immediately, and Mustang is shown to have survived his wounds despite collapsing from them, it can be assumed that he wouldn't have to wait too long to receive medical assistance (especially considering the Bradleys' escorts were also nearby). If Selim's spine was damaged, then the risk of permanent paralysis or death is much greater. Notably, when Pride strangles Selim, he grips him by and squeezes on the front of his neck, which would be the opposite side that his spinal cord is. Additionally, despite Selim having his eyes opened while being strangled, his eyes are seen to have closed by the time he is in Mustang's arms being carried out of the building. This actually points in favour of Selim having only lost consciousness and not dying instantly, as the muscles which let you keep your eyelids shut are controlled by the central nervous system, indicating his is still working. If he had died already, it would be much more likely that his eye muscles would relax and his eyelids would drift open.

Trivia

Selim Bradley's character design slightly resembles Suan, a character from Arakawa Hiromu's Shanghai Youma Kikai, the discontinued manga series Arakawa wrote earlier before Fullmetal Alchemist.
Selim's name comes from the Ottoman Sultan's name Selim I.
